title: Liquid methane storage and fuel delivery system
abstract: Systems and methods for the storage and delivery of fuel, such as methane. In certain aspects, a system is provided with an inner vessel, and outer vessel, and a rope suspension system connecting the two vessels. In certain aspects, the disclosed storage tanks operate at low pressure with long hold times, and have a non-cylindrical shape.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A storage tank (400), comprising: an outer vessel (404); an inner storage vessel (402) arranged within said outer vessel; and a support system (406) connecting said inner storage vessel to said outer vessel, wherein said support system comprises a rope suspension system connecting a first element (408) mounted said outer vessel with a second element (410) mounted on said inner storage vessel. 2. The storage tank of claim 1 , wherein said inner storage vessel is a cryogenic storage tank adapted to store liquid methane, and wherein said liquid methane is stored at a pressure in the range of 1-3 bar and with a hold time of at least 5 days. 3. A methane storage tank (400), comprising: an outer vessel (404); an inner storage vessel (402) arranged within said outer vessel and adapted to store liquid methane, wherein said inner storage vessel is square or a rounded rectangle in cross-section; and a support system connecting said inner storage vessel to said outer vessel. 4. The tank of any of claims 1-3, wherein said support system comprises rope made from aramid or para-aramid synthetic fibers. 5. The tank of claim 4, wherein said rope comprises a fixing loop formed by splicing. 6 The tank of any of claims 1-5, wherein said support system comprises one or more of a threaded tensioning arrangement and Belleville washers. 7. The tank of any of claims 1-6, wherein at least one of said inner and outer vessel comprises an ultrasonic transducer configured to provide data regarding a level of said methane in said inner storage vessel based an ultrasonic signal through said inner storage vessel; the tank further comprises a thermocouple fixedly attached to an outer surface of said inner storage vessel; or the tank further comprises an output line, wherein said output line has a pressure sensor. 8. The tank of any of claims 1-7, further comprising a control module 1600, wherein said control module is configured to send data relating to one or more of pressure, temperature, and methane level to a central control facility. 9. The tank of any of claims 1-8, wherein: said inner storage vessel is arranged within said outer vessel without a metal supporting collar, said support system is arranged to prevent the inner storage vessel from directly contacting said outer vessel, a space between said in and outer vessel is vacuum, and said support system comprises one or more of pulleys and wheels. 10. The tank of any of claims 1-9, wherein the storage tank is mounted in a vehicle and connected to a combustion engine, wherein said tank is further arranged to deliver said methane to said combustion engine. 11. A fuel delivery system (300), comprising: a storage tank (302); a heat exchanger (306) coupled to said storage tank, wherein said heat exchanger is configured to process methane extracted from said storage tank; a first compressor (310) coupled to said heat exchanger and configured to pressurize said methane processed by said heat exchanger; a power unit (304) coupled to said first compressor, wherein said power unit is configured to operate using said pressurized methane; a second compressor (311) coupled to said storage tank; and an auxiliary power unit (308) coupled to at least one of said power unit, said heat exchanger, and one or more of said compressors. 12. The fuel delivery system of claim 11, wherein said second compressor is arranged to act in parallel with said first compressor to deliver methane gas to the power unit on-demand, and wherein said second compressor is further arranged to operate independently from said first compressor to supply methane gas for storage in a high pressure buffer (314) or to drive a refrigeration circuit (316) of said storage tank. 13. The fuel delivery system of claims 11 or 12, wherein said power unit is a combustion engine of a vehicle, wherein said auxiliary power unit is configured to provide power and heat during a start-up of said vehicle or to provide power and heat to said vehicleâs quarters. 14. A method (1400) of assembling a methane storage tank, comprising placing (1402) an inner storage vessel within an outer vessel, wherein said inner storage vessel is adapted to store liquid methane; and attaching (1404) said inner storage vessel to said outer storage vessel using a support system, wherein said support system comprises a rope suspension system, wherein at least one of said inner and outer vessels have a non-cylindrical cross-section. 15. A method (1500) of operating a vehicle, comprising: filling (1502) a storage tank according to any of claims 1-10 with methane; and operating (1504) said vehicle with a combustion engine powered by said methane, wherein said storage tank comprises one or more of a rope suspension system and a storage vessel having a square or rounded rectangular shape in cross-section. 16. The tank of any of claims 1-10, wherein the storage tank has an ullage of 4% or less and is arranged to operate with a pressure in the range of 0-100 mbar. 17. A method (1550) of operating a vehicle, comprising: extracting (1552) methane from a low pressure storage tank; 

 generating (1556) pressurized methane by compressing said extracted methane; and operating (1558) a power unit of said vehicle using said pressurized methane, wherein said storage tank comprises one or more of a rope suspension system and a storage vessel having a square or rounded rectangular shape in cross-section. 18. The method of claim 17, further comprising: processing (1554) said extracted methane with a heat exchanger. 19. The method of claim 17 or 18, further comprising: delivering (1560) one or more of said extracted methane and said pressurized methane to a buffer; and passing (1562) methane stored in said buffer to said storage tank. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein said delivering comprises use of a pressure booster and second compressor. 21. The method of any of claims 17-20, further comprising: generating energy (1564) with an auxiliary power unit using methane bled from said storage tank; and performing (1566) one or more of heating a vehicle passenger area, operating said heat exchanger, and starting up said vehicle using said generated energy from the auxiliary power unit. 22. The method of any of claims 17-21, wherein one or more of said extracting (1552), processing (1554), generating pressurized methane (1556), delivering (1560), passing (1562), generating energy (1564), and performing (1566) are in response to a demand for gaseous methane. 23. The method of claim 21 or 22, wherein said methane bled from said storage tank is one or more of said methane stored in said buffer and said methane processed by said heat exchanger.
